SnowDistribution: Hard snow ice extensive especially where exposed to the wind.  Cloudy on the tops with some fleeting bright spells at first, with visibility decreasing during the afternoon. Winds 20 - 30 mph easing through the day.
WeatherInfluences: There will be small amounts of fresh snow above 650 metres around dawn. This will become more persistent from midday with snow falling to 200 metres by the end of the period. Light South-Westerlies veer to the North-West becoming strong by the afternoon. The freezing level will gradually lower from 800 metres to 400 metres.
SnowStability: Unstable windslab will be found on mainly North-West to North-East aspects above 900 metres. Gully tops, coire rims and crag aprons will be most affected. During the day, shallow accumulations of isolated fresh windslab will start to build on East and South-East aspects. Cornices will also be found on the same aspects. Below this height, instabilities will be found in steep wind sheltered locations. Wind exposed areas will be stable, firm and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight snow cleared to leave a dry day with a few isolated snow showers. Winds from the Southern quadrant eased through the day. The freezing level hovered around 800 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of unstable windslab has developed in steep wind sheltered locations on mainly North-West to North-East aspects above 950 metres, with some cross-loading taking place. Gully tops, coire rims and crag aprons most affected. Fragile cornices are present on the same aspects. Many areas are stable, firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Good cover above 700 metres, hard going in many places on foot.  A day of flat light and no definition. Winds 20-30 mph.
Comment: Poor visibility and tricky navigation at higher levels on Sunday, similar expected on Monday.
WeatherInfluences: Southerly winds will veer North-West through the period. Overnight snow showers will ease through the day. The freezing level will be around 600 metres.
SnowStability: The existing windslab will consolidate slightly. However, fresh unstable deposits will accumulate overnight and during the day. Significant accumulations will be found in sheltered locations above 900 metres mainly, but not exclusively, on North to East aspects. Fresh cornices above these aspects will be unstable. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight snow turned showery through the day. The freezing level was generally around 1000 metres although sometimes dropping to 700 metres during snow showers. Southerly winds veered to the South-West and eased through the day.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh areas of unstable windslab have been deposited on mainly North-West to North-East aspects above 900 metres. Fresh cornices above these aspects are unstable. Below 900 metres the snowpack tends to be damp and stability is better.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Existing snow firm with softer accumulations, generally above 600 metres.  Cloudy on the tops with some very brief brighter spells.  Winds approx 10mph to 20mph.
Comment: Remaining wintery with Northerly winds, calmer and dryer on Tuesday.
WeatherInfluences: Wintery showers continue through the period falling as snow above around 600 metres overnight and to progressively lower elevations through the day, around 200 metres. Moderate winds from the South overnight veer to the North and increase through the day becoming fresh to strong.
SnowStability: The existing snowpack will be generally firm and stable with some fresh snow accumulations. Unstable windslab will be present in steep, wind sheltered locations on mainly North-West to North-East aspects above 700 metres with coire rims, gully tops and ridge flanks most affected. Cornices will be present in these locations also. During the day fresh deposits of unstable windslab will begin to form on more Easterly and Southerly aspects. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Brief showers falling as snow above 850 metres this morning becoming drier but remaining cloudy over the summits. Winds were moderate to fresh from the South.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack is generally firm and stable with areas of fresh wet snow. Above 850 metres fresh accumulations of unstable wind slab are present in steep wind sheltered locations mainly on North-West to North-East aspects. Areas most affected are coire rims, gully tops and ridge flanks. Cornices are also present in these locations. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: New snow above 400 metres.  Winds approx 10 - 15 mph. Poor visibility above 800 metres.
Comment: Stability will be generally good on exposed slopes with a cover of soft snow on a firm and icy base.
WeatherInfluences: New snow overnight and on Monday will continue to affect slopes mainly above 600 metres. Winds will be moderate to fresh, generally South-Westerly overnight but veering to the North and strengthening to occasionally strong through the course of the day. The freezing level will be around 650 metres.
SnowStability: New snow and wind transport of existing snow will form weakly bonded windslab in wind sheltered locations. Greatest accumulations are expected to form above 850 metres in sheltered gullies and on slopes with a mainly North-West through North to Easterly aspect. Cross loading may affect other aspects. Fragile cornices may exist above similar areas. On more exposed slopes stability will be generally good with a cover of new snow on a firm and icy base. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: New snow overnight and today has affected slopes above 400 metres. Winds have been generally Southerly, strong overnight becoming moderate today. After a cool night the freezing level has risen to around 900 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of windslab exist in sheltered gullies and on slopes with a mainly North-Westerly through North to North-Easterly aspect above 900 metres. Cross loading may have affected other slopes. On exposed slopes, a light cover of new snow exists on a firm and icy base. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Fresh snow at all altitudes - hard going on foot in places.  Poor visibility throughout the day above 600 metres. Winds approx 10 - 20 mph fresh, cooling.
Comment: Forecast to be colder and drier through Tuesday and Wednesday.
WeatherInfluences: Snow showers above 600 metres overnight, then a drier interlude before further snow in the afternoon. The freezing level will fluctuate around 650 metres initially, decreasing from midday to 400 metres. Moderate South-Westerly winds will veer North-North-West throughout the day while increasing to become fresh.
SnowStability: Unstable windslab will persist on North-West through North-East to South-East aspects above 850 metres. Areas most affected will be steep wind sheltered terrain such as coire rims and gully tops. Fragile cornices will be present above the same aspects. Stability better at lower elevations and in places exposed to the wind. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Heavy snowfall overnight became showery through the day. Following a cold night, the freezing level rose to around 750 metres. Light to moderate Southerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Overnight fresh snow to glen level, however at lower elevations this was thawing throughout the day. Unstable windslab is present on steep North-West through North to East aspects above 800 metres. Fragile cornices overhang many of these locations. Generally good stability on windward aspects and ridges. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Overnight snow to glen level, trail breaking required. No visibility above 800 metres. Very cold wind chill.
Comment: Firm and icy conditions underfoot. Crampons required to make safe and efficient progress on wind exposed slopes.
WeatherInfluences: Dry overnight and through most of the day, with very light snowfall arriving by mid-afternoon. Moderate North-North-Westerly winds overnight, will steadily increase to strong by the end of the period. Freezing level generally around 600 metres, with a brief rise to 700 metres, then lowering to 400 metres by late afternoon.
SnowStability: Localised moderately to weakly bonded windslab will persist in steeper wind sheltered locations on West through North to North-East aspects above 900 metres. Gully tops, steep scarp slopes and around corrie rims will be worst affected. Localised areas of moderately bonded snow will be encountered below this altitude and occasionally on wind sheltered South-West and East aspects. Exposed slopes will be scoured and icy.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight snowfall to glen level eased first thing to give a mainly dry but cloudy day, until late afternoon when further snowfall arrived above 600 metres. Severe gale force South and South-South-Easterly winds overnight, eased to fresh South-South-Westerly during the day. Freezing to glen level overnight, rising to around 800 metres by mid-afternoon, then lowering again.
ObservedSnowStability: Snowfall and drifting deposits have formed moderately to weakly bonded windslab in wind sheltered locations on West through North to North-East aspects above 900 metres. Steep slopes, corrie rims and gully tops are worst affected. Fragile cornices have formed. Moderately bonded windslab is present below this altitude and on some South-West and East aspects. Wind exposed slopes are scoured and icy.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
